answer:
This passage appears in Johann Gottlieb Fichte's *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit von den Fürsten Europens* (A Reclamation of Freedom of Thought from the Princes of Europe), Chapter 2.[^1]

> you point there to a gentle people, sunk to the fury of cannibals, how it thirsts for blood, and not for tears, how it presses more eagerly to executions than to actors, how it carries around torn limbs of its fellow citizens, still dripping and steaming, amidst songs of jubilation, how its children spin bloody heads instead of tops—and we do not want to remind you of bloodier festivals that despotism and fanaticism, in their usual alliance, gave to this very people—not remind you that these are not the fruits of freedom of thought, but the consequences of previous long intellectual slavery[^1]

[^1]: Johann Gottlieb Fichte, *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit von den Fürsten Europens*, [paragraph](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02/translation/#p-659c32).
